message:
UI: disable text wrapping in search bar for cursor visibility

Remove Wrap configuration from search bar paragraph widget to fix cursor
positioning issues on long queries.

Problem:
When text wrapping was enabled, typing a long query that exceeded the
search bar width would cause the cursor indicator (▎) at the end of the
input to wrap to a new line. However, since the search bar has a fixed
height, the wrapped cursor would be clipped, making it invisible to the
user. This created a confusing UX where users couldn't see their cursor
position while typing long queries.

Solution:
Disable wrapping so the text extends past the visible area. The cursor
remains on the same line as the text, staying visible at the rightmost
position. While this means long queries extend off-screen to the left,
the cursor and most recent characters remain visible.

Future improvement:
TODO comment added for implementing proper horizontal scrolling that
keeps the cursor and surrounding context visible while allowing the
user to see the full query through scrolling.

Also removes unused Wrap import since it's no longer used.
